conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've been feeling really sad for weeks. I can't sleep, I've lost my appetite, and I have no energy.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: I'm sorry to hear you're going through this. How long has this been going on?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: About 6 weeks. I've also lost interest in things I used to enjoy. I don't see the point.

routing_reason: Classic depression symptoms: anhedonia, sleep disturbance, appetite loss, low energy lasting >2 weeks

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I worry constantly. About work, about my family, about money. I can't turn my brain off.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: That sounds exhausting. How long have you been experiencing this?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: For months. I also feel restless and irritable all the time. My muscles are tense.

routing_reason: Excessive worry, restlessness, irritability, muscle tension — hallmark GAD-7 indicators

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've been working 80-hour weeks and I feel completely burned out.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: That sounds like an enormous amount of pressure. How are you coping day to day?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Not well. I feel overwhelmed, detached, and unable to concentrate. Everything feels like too much.

routing_reason: Overwhelm, detachment, cognitive difficulties — stress overload indicators; PSS-10 appropriate for burnout assessment

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I was in a road accident 3 months ago. I keep having nightmares about it.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: That sounds really traumatic. Are there other ways the accident has been affecting you?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Yes. I avoid driving now. I get flashbacks randomly during the day. I feel numb and detached.

routing_reason: Trauma exposure + nightmares + avoidance + flashbacks + emotional numbing = PTSD symptom cluster (DSM-5 criteria B/C/D)

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I have a drink every evening to unwind. I think it's become a habit.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: I appreciate you sharing that. How much would you typically drink on those evenings?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Maybe 3-4 drinks. Sometimes more on weekends. I've been told I drink too much but I don't think it's a problem.

routing_reason: Regular alcohol use, quantity suggesting hazardous drinking, denial of problem — AUDIT for full screening, CAGE-AID for quick dependence check

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've been feeling depressed and anxious at the same time. I don't know which one it is.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Both can occur together. Can you tell me more about what you're experiencing?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Persistent sadness, constant worry, and I'm so stressed I can't function. It's all mixed up.

routing_reason: Mixed depression, anxiety, and stress presentation — DASS-21 measures all three subscales simultaneously

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've completed 8 therapy sessions and feel significantly better.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: That's wonderful progress. How are you finding your day-to-day wellbeing compared to when we started?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Much better. I feel more optimistic, connected with people, and purposeful.

routing_reason: Treatment progress monitoring — WEMWBS measures positive wellbeing to track therapeutic gains

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've been using sleeping tablets every night for 6 months. I tried to stop but I couldn't.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Can you tell me more about the tablets? Are they prescribed?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: They were prescribed but I'm taking more than prescribed. I also use alcohol to help me sleep on some nights.

routing_reason: Substance dependence indicators (tolerance, inability to stop, mixing substances) — CAGE-AID for rapid dependence screening

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: My UPSC exam is in 2 months. I've been studying 16 hours a day.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: That's an intense schedule. How are you feeling physically and mentally?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Exhausted. I feel like I'm going to break. I can't stop even if I want to.

routing_reason: High stress, exhaustion, loss of perceived control — PSS-10 appropriate for academic stress quantification

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've been experiencing low mood for a while but I'm managing okay day to day.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: I'm glad you're coping. Would you like to do a quick check-in to see where you are?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Sure, that might be useful to have a baseline.

routing_reason: Low-grade mood concerns with adequate functioning — PHQ-9 for clinical baseline, WEMWBS for wellbeing contrast

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I witnessed domestic violence as a child. I've never processed it.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Thank you for trusting me with this. Has it been affecting you in your adult life?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Yes. I startle easily, I have trust issues, and I sometimes feel like I'm reliving it.

routing_reason: Childhood trauma exposure + hypervigilance + re-experiencing + trust issues = developmental trauma presentation requiring PTSD screen

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I feel fine most days. I'm here because my company recommended it.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: That's great your company supports this. Would you like to start with a wellbeing check?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Sure, I'm open to it. I don't have any major issues.

routing_reason: No clinical indicators — wellbeing baseline appropriate for company-referred users with no presenting issues

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've been having dark thoughts. Not quite suicidal but I sometimes wish I didn't exist.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: I hear you. These kinds of thoughts can be very distressing. Are they intrusive or do they feel more like wishes?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: More like passive wishes. I wouldn't act on it. I just feel tired of everything.

routing_reason: Passive suicidal ideation (PHQ-9 item 9 trigger) + depressive features — PHQ-9 mandatory, item 9 score will inform crisis protocol

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I'm worried I might have a drinking problem. I drink to cope with stress.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Thank you for being open about this. How often would you say you drink?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Most days. Sometimes I get drunk at work events and regret it. I feel like I can't control it.

routing_reason: Daily drinking + loss of control + regret after drinking — AUDIT for severity, CAGE-AID for dependence indicators

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I'm feeling somewhat stressed from the new project.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Project pressure can be tough. Is it affecting other areas of your life?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Not really. Just work hours are longer. I'm coping otherwise.

routing_reason: Mild situational stress with adequate coping — no clinical threshold indicators; monitor in next session

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I'm a new mother and I've been feeling really low and disconnected from my baby.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: That sounds really hard. When did these feelings start?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: About 3 weeks after delivery. I feel guilty for not bonding properly. I cry a lot.

routing_reason: Postpartum onset, emotional disconnection, guilt, crying — PHQ-9 for postpartum depression screening (Edinburgh variant also applicable)

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've just returned from working abroad for 3 years. I feel out of place everywhere.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Reverse culture shock can be very real. How are you managing day to day?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: With difficulty. I feel disconnected from family and friends. My sense of self feels fragmented.

routing_reason: Reverse culture shock / identity disruption — wellbeing and stress baseline appropriate; no acute clinical indicators

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I think I might be using cannabis too much. It's daily now.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Daily use can sometimes become a pattern that's hard to change. Has it affected your life?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: Yes. My motivation is low. I smoke to feel normal. I get anxious without it.

routing_reason: Daily substance use + psychological dependence + anxiety without substance = CAGE-AID primary; GAD-7 to assess underlying anxiety disorder

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I feel great today. I've been making a lot of progress in therapy.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: That's wonderful to hear. What's been making the biggest difference?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I feel more confident, more connected to people, and more purposeful in my work.

routing_reason: Strong positive indicators across life domains — no clinical assessment needed; wellbeing gains being demonstrated

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: I've been getting headaches and can't sleep. I'm under a lot of pressure at work.

conversation_context.speaker: assistant
conversation_context.text: Somatic stress symptoms are common when pressure is very high. How long has this been going on?

conversation_context.speaker: user
conversation_context.text: About a month. I feel overwhelmed and unable to relax even on weekends.

routing_reason: Somatic stress (headache, insomnia), sustained pressure, inability to relax — PSS-10 for stress severity; GAD-7 to rule out anxiety disorder
